result.title: fix(player): Video player close button hidden behind navbar on scroll
result.summary:
When users open the video player and have previously scrolled down the page, the navbar's backdrop blur overlaps the top-right corner of the player modal, partially obscuring the close button.

**What's happening:** Session recordings show users opening the video player from movie detail pages after scrolling down. The fixed navbar (z-50) renders its blurred background over the player modal's close button area. Users resort to pressing Escape or clicking the backdrop to close the player instead of using the close button.

**Root cause:** The navbar in `app/components/Navbar.tsx` uses `z-50` (z-index: 50), while the Player modal in `app/components/Player.tsx` uses `z-[60]`. Although z-[60] is higher than z-50, the navbar's `backdrop-blur-md` filter creates a new stacking context that visually overlaps the close button area. The issue is cosmetic — the player is still functional and closeable via Escape key or backdrop click.

**How to resolve:** Increase the player's z-index from `z-[60]` to `z-[100]` to ensure clear separation from the navbar's stacking context, or add `z-[70]` to the close button specifically.

relevant_code_paths: app/components/Player.tsx (line 38: `z-[60]` on the player modal overlay)
app/components/Navbar.tsx (line 32: `z-50` on the fixed navbar header)

relevant_commit_hashes.198632a: Set player z-index to z-[60] which is only marginally above the navbar z-50

findings.data_queried: Reviewed 12 session recordings where users opened the video player. In 4 of them, users visibly moved their cursor toward the close button area, paused, then used the Escape key instead. The overlap is subtle — only the top-right corner of the close button is partially covered by the navbar blur.

relevant_commit_hashes.198632a: Both z-index values were set in the same commit

findings.data_queried: This is a minor visual glitch. The player is still fully functional — users can close via Escape key, clicking the dark backdrop, or clicking the non-overlapped portion of the close button. No errors are thrown.

actionability.explanation: Simple CSS z-index bump from z-[60] to z-[100] on the Player component's root div. The fix is a single class change with no side effects. However, this is a minor cosmetic issue — the player is fully functional via alternative close methods (Escape key, backdrop click).

priority.explanation: Minor visual glitch affecting the close button on the video player modal. Users can still close the player via Escape or backdrop click. Low user impact, cosmetic only.
